title: moquiniastrum sordidum
intro: Moquiniastrum sordidum, also known as the "palo amarillo" or yellow stick, is a type of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Latin America, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, among others. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Moquiniastrum sordidum are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
